Label: Low
Note: Tenant activity is unusually quiet, with rent paid on time and minimal management intervention needed.

Label: Normal
Note: Tenant communication and day-to-day issues are typical for a property like yours, with occasional routine matters handled through standard practices.

Label: High
Note: Tenant-related issues are occurring more than expected — repeated complaints, payment follow-up, conflicts, or lease-enforcement needs beyond the ordinary.

Label: Severe
Note: Tenant activity is materially disruptive relative to a typical property — active evictions, legal disputes, serious violations, or persistent conflict.

Label: Low
Note: Turnover is unusually low — tenants are staying long-term, vacancies are rare, and leasing effort is minimal.

Label: Normal
Note: Unit turnover is occurring at a typical pace for this property type and market, handled through ordinary leasing activity.

Label: High
Note: Turnover on this property is running higher than typical, requiring more frequent leasing, marketing, and rent-ready turnaround than a standard property in this market.

Label: Severe
Note: Turnover is a major operating problem here — multiple vacancies, chronic churn, or prolonged difficulty re-leasing units.

Label: Low
Note: Maintenance needs are unusually light — the property is in strong condition and service calls are rare.

Label: Normal
Note: Maintenance needs are routine for a property of this age, size, and condition — ordinary service calls and upkeep.

Label: High
Note: Maintenance demand is running above normal — recurring repairs, aging systems, and more frequent vendor coordination than a typical property.

Label: Severe
Note: Maintenance represents a major operating burden — serious deferred maintenance, safety issues, major system failures, or a large repair backlog.

Label: Low
Note: Compliance and administrative requirements are unusually simple — no special rules, added filings, or elevated compliance tasks.

Label: Normal
Note: Compliance and administrative requirements are typical for this jurisdiction and property type — standard notices and recordkeeping, with no unusual filings or oversight expected.

Label: High
Note: Compliance work is running above normal — rent-control tracking, recurring inspections, registration requirements, or HOA coordination beyond the ordinary.

Label: Severe
Note: Compliance and regulatory exposure here is significant — active code enforcement, lawsuits, complex local restrictions, or unresolved notices.
